name: Recent review places vaccine-response work at the mTOR-microbiota boundary
summary: A 2026 review supports recent-review and taxonomy-boundary coverage by separating immunosenescence mechanisms from direct efficacy claims.
statement: A 2026 review frames aging vaccine efficacy as an interaction among immunosenescence, inflammaging, gut microbiota, and mTOR signaling, while treating mTOR inhibitors, probiotics, and dietary strategies as promising but not yet definitive human efficacy interventions.
caveats: This is review and taxonomy context, not a new interventional efficacy result.
The review combines preclinical mechanisms, human correlations, and intervention hypotheses.
It reinforces that immune-rejuvenation records must remain separated from mTOR, microbiome, diet, and vaccine-product efficacy claims.
